question_text: Let f : R →R be a twice-differentiable function and suppose its second derivative satisﬁes f ′′(x) > 0 for all x ∈R. Which of the following statements is/are ALWAYS

options.B: There does not exist x and y, x ̸= y, such that f ′(x) = f ′(y) = 0
options.D: f has at most one local minimum
